# opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map (Resource)

Configure OSPF route maps.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map" "example" {
  name         = "OSPF_RM"
  action       = "permit"
  route_map_id = "10"
  set          = "metric 100"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) Set permit for match or deny to negate the rule. One of `"permit"`, `"deny"`.
- `name` (String) The name of this route map.
- `route_map_id` (String) The route map ID (sequence number).

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable this route map. Defaults to `true`.
- `prefix_list` (String) UUID of the prefix list to match. Defaults to `""`.
- `set` (String) Free text field for set commands. Defaults to `""`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the OSPF route map.

## Import

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map using the `id`. For example:

```terraform
import {
  to = opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map.example
  id = "<opnsense-resource-id>"
}
```

Using `terraform import`, import opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map using the `id`. For example:

```console
% terraform import opnsense_quagga_ospf_route_map.example <opnsense-resource-id>
```
